analysis_description: Tape-based IV (Brentq from mid vs extract mid), model IV from global quadratic smile in m=log(K/S)/sqrt(T), BS vega; L1 spread means; vega_rail grid mapping rail constant to max |pos| per strike at mid-session sample. TTE/DTE: round3work/round3description.txt (historical day index maps to TTE at open 8,7,6 for days 0,1,2 per spec example pattern) plus intraday DTE_eff = DTE_open - (timestamp//100)/10000 per round3work/plotting/original_method/combined_analysis/plot_iv_smile_round3.py (T = DTE_eff/365 years).
used_to_decide: Informed vega_rail magnitude (order-of-hundreds in vega proxy per unit position) and confirmed ATM-focused behavior is safer than flooding deep strikes (see iteration 1). Smile coeffs reused from round3work/voucher_work/5200_work/calibration.json global fit.

analysis_description: Implicit parameter sweep from backtests: IV_SCALPING_THR vs wing PnL (v1 low gate -> large losses on VEV_4000/4500); v2 ATM-only + no flatten reduces trade count but does not fix extract drag.
used_to_decide: Reverted toward stricter Frankfurt gate; kept order merge from v1 as infrastructure fix in v3; next iterations should parent v3 and tune day-2 hydrogel/extract risk or scalp thresholds narrowly.

analysis_description: IV/Greek signal-frequency by strike bucket (ATM/mid/wings) from ROUND_3 tapes using smile-IV BS theo/vega and Frankfurt theo-diff EMA windows. Compared edge trigger hit rates at THR_OPEN=0.45 and switch statistics to explain sparse fills.
used_to_decide: Observed ATM has materially tighter spreads and higher edge-hit rates than wings while wings have very wide spreads and low trigger rates; this justified pivoting away from underlying/hydro MM drag and testing pure vega-rail voucher execution (v4/v5).

analysis_description: Interpreting v6/v7 in light of vega_signal_window_stats.json: ATM q75 switch is ~0.27 (below Frankfurt gate 0.7), so most of the time the quiet flatten branch or no-fill dominates; v7 lowered IV gate toward q75 to increase use of the main branch but reintroduced small wing/extract hedge cost (VEV_5500, extract), net negative vs v5.
used_to_decide: Best candidate remains v5 (+14 total PnL). v6 ATM filter kept strategy safe but thinned to +2. Next parents: v5 for threshold tuning around switch_mean and THR_OPEN without dropping IV gate as far as v1.

analysis_description: Subsampled tape distribution of the Frankfurt switch_mean (EMA_100 of |theo_diff - EMA_20|) per voucher row; TTE and smile as traders. Output: share of rows where switch_mean >= 0.55/0.65/0.7 (rare) and quantiles 0.5-0.99. This explains why IV_SCALPING_THR=0.7 rarely opens the main branch and why v8 (no extract hedge) ties v5 on total PnL.
used_to_decide: v8 duplicate of v5 PnL (+14) with no extract: hedge was not the edge driver. v9 (slightly looser gate) was worse (-8) due to extract hedge drag + more wing strikes. Prefer v5 or v8 as parent; if tuning IV gate, use small steps above tape median (~0.23) and below 0.55 unless willing to change EMA window shape.

analysis_description: v10 set IV gate to 0.25 to align with subsampled switch_mean median (~0.23). That let the *main* Frankfurt scalper run on all strikes, not the quiet-flatten path — wings (VEV_4000/4500) dominated losses. v11 kept gate 0.7 but set QUIET_FLATTEN off: when switch_mean is low, no more forced take/stop of full inventory at the touch (reduces spread-driven hidden liquidation PnL). v11 PnL +96 vs v8 +14, consistent with prior v2 ‘no quiet flatten’ direction but now with v8’s no-hedge baseline.
used_to_decide: Do **not** use tape median as IV activity gate for the *main* branch without strike filters — it replicates v1-style wing damage. Next parents: v11; optional combine with ATM filter or slightly higher gate than 0.25 for any main-branch experiments.

analysis_description: ATM vs non-ATM switch/edge analysis from ROUND_3 tapes using trader-consistent smile+TTE+EMA formulas (output atm_switch_vs_edge.json). ATM subset shows much tighter spreads and stronger edge-open hit rates than non-ATM, while high switch_mean remains rare overall; this supports combining no-quiet-flatten with ATM-only exposure.
used_to_decide: Guided v12 (ATM filter + no quiet flatten) which improved PnL to +130. v13 showed over-tight vega rail sacrifices too much edge; keep rail near 650 with ATM filter.
